audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar seventh-month offering calendar, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for trumpet/blast day, Day of Atonement, seven-day Booths offerings, descending bull sequence, eighth-day assembly, and relationship to continual, vowed, and freewill offerings.

verses.restored: In seventh month, on first of month, holy convocation shall be for you; no laborious work you shall do. A day of trumpet-blast it shall be for you.
You shall make burnt offering for soothing scent to YHWH: one young bull, one ram, seven complete male lambs, sons of a year;
and their grain offering, fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths for bull, two-tenths for ram,
and one-tenth for each lamb of seven lambs;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, to make atonement/covering for you;

besides burnt offering of new moon and its grain offering, and continual burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings according to their judgment, for soothing scent, fire-offering to YHWH.
On tenth day of this seventh month holy convocation shall be for you, and you shall humble/afflict your souls/lives; no work you shall do.
You shall bring near burnt offering to YHWH, soothing scent: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year; complete they shall be for you;
and their grain offering, fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths for bull, two-tenths for one ram,
one-tenth for each lamb of seven lambs;
one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides sin/purification offering of atonement/covering and continual burnt offering and its grain offering, with their drink offerings.
On fifteenth day of seventh month holy convocation shall be for you; no laborious work you shall do, and you shall celebrate festival to YHWH seven days.
You shall bring near burnt offering, fire-offering of soothing scent to YHWH: thirteen young bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs, sons of a year; complete they shall be.
Their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with oil, three-tenths for each bull of thirteen bulls, two-tenths for each ram of two rams,
and one-tenth for each lamb of fourteen lambs;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
On second day: twelve young bulls, two rams, fourteen complete male lambs, sons of a year;
and their grain offering and drink offerings for bulls, rams, and lambs by their number, according to judgment;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering and its grain offering, with their drink offerings.
On third day: eleven bulls, two rams, fourteen complete male lambs, sons of a year;
and their grain offering and drink offerings for bulls, rams, and lambs by their number, according to judgment;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
On fourth day: ten bulls, two rams, fourteen complete male lambs, sons of a year;
their grain offering and drink offerings for bulls, rams, and lambs by their number, according to judgment;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
On fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, fourteen complete male lambs, sons of a year;
and their grain offering and drink offerings for bulls, rams, and lambs by their number, according to judgment;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
On sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, fourteen complete male lambs, sons of a year;
and their grain offering and drink offerings for bulls, rams, and lambs by their number, according to judgment;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offerings.
On seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, fourteen complete male lambs, sons of a year;
and their grain offering and drink offerings for bulls, rams, and lambs by their number, according to their judgment;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
On eighth day solemn assembly shall be for you; no laborious work you shall do.
You shall bring near burnt offering, fire-offering of soothing scent to YHWH: one bull, one ram, seven complete male lambs, sons of a year;
their grain offering and drink offerings for bull, ram, and lambs by their number, according to judgment;
and one male goat as sin/purification offering, besides continual burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
These you shall make to YHWH at your appointed times, besides your vows and freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings, grain offerings, drink offerings, and peace/well-being offerings.
Moses spoke to children of Israel according to all YHWH commanded Moses.

translation_notes: The first-day trumpet/blast observance is textually a sacred rest and blast memorial; later Jewish tradition receives it as Rosh Hashanah.
The tenth day is the Day of Atonement. Humbling/afflicting the self is traditionally expressed through fasting but must never become self-injury, eating-disorder coercion, or medical neglect.
The fifteenth-day festival is Sukkot/Booths, followed by an eighth-day solemn assembly.
The Booths bull offerings descend from thirteen through seven across seven days, then shift to one bull on the eighth day.
These sanctuary offerings supplement rather than replace the daily offering, vows, freewill gifts, and the broader festival meanings in Leviticus 23.
The seventh-month calendar remains living Jewish sacred time and must not be superseded or appropriated.
safety_note: Do not reenact animal sacrifice, coerce unsafe fasting, or use these festivals to erase Jewish continuity. The chapter preserves ancient sanctuary service within a calendar of awakening, atonement, harvest joy, and solemn gathering.
